observations.recommendation: Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General Services and the Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property, pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We recommend the Management to: a. Instruct the GSO to keep and maintain Stock Cards for each and every supply to have an adequate monitoring and immediate information of supplies on hand; b. Require the Accounting Division to keep and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards to record supplies received, issued and the balance both in quantity and amount; c. Ensure proper coordination between GSO and Accounting Division to have an updated and reconciled balances in their Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively; and d. Prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in compliance with Section 121 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We recommend the Municipality to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for the first and second semesters, not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, respectively, in compliance to Section 124 of Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee prepares the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) with the approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester and the Annual Procurement Plan to GPPB on or before end of January of the budget year.

1.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), faithfully:
a) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count
of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than
January 31, of each year;
b) Instruct the GSO to maintain and update his property cards per category of
property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update his property ledger
cards per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume I; and
d) Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant reconcile
their property cards and property ledger cards, respectively.

2.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement on a
monthly basis in accordance with Section 74 of the Presidential Decree No.
1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October
2, 1996 and submit regularly to the auditor for review and verification.

3.5 We recommended the Municipality to submit the Report on the
Physical Count of Inventories for the first and second semesters, not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year, respectively, in compliance to
Section 124 of Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs.

4.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to record the appropriate accounts. It is
further recommended that future transactions be analyzed and
classified as to the prescribed accounts in Section 1(2) of the COA Circular
No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in order to avoid
misstatements and to fairly present the balances in the financial
statements.

5.5 Therefore, we highly recommended that the LCE to:
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately
an extensive inventory of the road network system under the
Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every
road network based on the result of the physical count;
c) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously
maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
d) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
e) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

6.9 We highly recommended that the LCE to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all regular
procurements of supplies in their respective inventory accounts,
record the same as expense when issuance thereof takes place,
and maintain the appropriate ledgers;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the
necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards; and
c) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the
ledgers on a regular basis.

7.7 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reclassify the PPE accounts below ₱50,000.00 threshold to its appropriate
semi-expendable properties and expenses; and
b. Adjust the opening balance in the Statement of Changes in Net
Assets/Equity in compliance to the COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated May
31, 2022.

8.5 We recommended to the General Services Officer to facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying ICS of the semi-expendable properties,
pursuant to Section 4.1 of the COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated May 31,
2022.

9.5 We strongly recommended the LCE:
a) To supervise and urge the assigned personnel to comply with the above-
mentioned provisions of the COA’s Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of
Government Motor Vehicles;
b) To established clear guidelines and protocols on the proper usage of
government vehicles, including the detailed procedures on the requisition and
issuance of fuel to the authorized officials. The regular issuance of driver’s trip
tickets be religiously complied with the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption
to be submitted for audit; and
c) Designate an employee who will be assigned to prepare, monitor and submit
the required Monthly Fuel Consumption Report.

Shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

To direct all Revenue Collector to prepare RAAF while the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Consolidated RAAF and submit to the Unit Auditor using the prescribed form provided in the Manual on New Government Accounting System not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month, pursuant to the Sections 50 and 51 of the said Manual; and
a. Reconcile the variance between the monthly deductions and monthly remittances
observations.managementAction: and b. Review the monthly deductions and ensure that the remittance of monthly premiums are accurate/exact amount of contribution withheld.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management to ensure that all cancelled checks with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein were submitted to the Commission on Audit for validation and monitoring, in compliance to Section 59 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We recommend that management should exercise prudence in incurring expenses and appropriately identify these expenses using the correct accounts in Volume III of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs). Refrain from disbursing funds not appropriated for Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses but rather provide funds for other accounts.

We recommend to the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the issuance of the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) of the properties, pursuant to Section 123, Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for LGUs.

Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General Services and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property, pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.
The Municipal Government should prepare inventory of local roads and submit supporting documentation in accordance with the cited COA circulars and MNGAS requirements.

1.9 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment, in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual
on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government
Units (LGUs), Volume I;
b) General Services Officer shall maintain and update his property cards per
category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume I;
c) Municipal Accountant shall maintain and update his property ledger cards
per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Section 120 of
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
d) Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant shall
reconcile their property cards and property ledger cards, respectively; and
e) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical
count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team
not later than January 31, of each year.

2.7 Therefore, we highly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation
that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank
Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance
with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

3.7 We recommended the management:
1. To direct all Revenue Collector to prepare RAAF while the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Consolidated RAAF and
submit to the Unit Auditor the said monthly reports using the
prescribed form provided in the Manual on New Government
Accounting System not later than the 5th day of the ensuing
month, pursuant to the Sections 50 and 51 of the said Manual;
and
2. Explain the variance/s between the records and the actual
inventory of accountable forms.

4.5 We recommend the Municipality to:
a. Reconcile the variance between the monthly deductions and
monthly remittances; and
b. Review the monthly deductions and ensure that the remittance
of monthly premiums are accurate/exact amount of contribution withheld.

5.4 We recommend that the Management to ensure that all cancelled
checks with the word “Cancelled” clearly indicated therein were
submitted to the Commission on Audit for validation and monitoring,
in compliance to Section 59 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs),
Volume I.

6.12 We recommend the Management to:
a. Instruct the GSO to keep and maintain Stock Cards for each and
every supply to have an adequate monitoring and immediate
information of supplies on hand;
b. Require the Accounting Division to keep and maintain Supplies
Ledger Cards to record supplies received, issued and the balance
both in quantity and amount;
c. Ensure proper coordination between GSO and Accounting
Division to have an updated and reconciled balances in their Stock
Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively; and
d. Prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in compliance with Section
121 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for
Local Government Units, Volume I.

7.5 We recommend the Municipality to submit the Report on the Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) for the first and second semesters, not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, respectively, in
compliance to Section 124 of Volume I of the Manual on New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Units (LGUs).

8.7 We recommend that the Bids and Award Committee prepares the
Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) with the approval by the Head of the
Procuring Entity and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester and
the Annual Procurement Plan to GPPB on or before end of January of the
budget year.

9.4 We recommend that management should exercise prudence in
incurring expenses and appropriately identify these expenses using
the correct accounts in Volume III of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Units (LGUs). Refrain from disbursing funds not appropriated for Other
Maintenance and Operating Expenses but rather provide funds for other accounts.

10.4 We recommend to the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying Acknowledgement Receipt for
Equipment (ARE) of the properties, pursuant to Section 123, Volume I of
the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for
Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned Accountable Officers to expedite the conduct of ocular verification of the above stated projects for us to ascertain their validity and existence, in compliance to the provision of Section 44 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

1. Furnish the Audit Team with the copies of the executed contracts and their supporting documents within the prescribed period; and
2. Henceforth, ensure that copies of these documents are given to the Audit Team within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated, February 12, 2009.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal DRRM Officer to prepare and submit the required reports pursuant to RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
The Head of Agency shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation for 2020.

We recommend to the management to compute and record the depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment.
Compute the depreciation using straight line method and depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life as provided under COA Circular 2017-004 dated December 13, 2017.

We recommend to the Municipality to open a Bank Account for the SEF, in compliance to Section 4(b) One Fund Concept of the Manual of New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I and Sections 307, 308, 309 and 310 of R.A. No. 7160.

The Municipal accountant must set-up a receivable account for its expected revenue collection for a given year. The assessment on the real property taxes collections from the Office of the Municipal Assessor serves as basis of the receivable for a year. The budget officer should furnish a copy of the budget for the year to the municipal accountant for setting-up receivable, pursuant to Section 20 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
We recommended the management to enforce immediate settlement of the issued audit suspensions within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned Accountable Officers to expedite the conduct of ocular verification of the above stated projects for us to ascertain their validity and existence, in compliance to the provision of Section 44 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman:
1. Furnish the Audit Team with the copies of the executed contracts and their supporting documents within the prescribed period; and
2. Henceforth, ensure that copies of these documents are given to the Audit Team within the prescribed period, pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated, February 12, 2009.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal DRRM Officer to prepare and submit the required reports pursuant to RA No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

The Head of Agency shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend to the management to compute and record the depreciation of Property, Plant and Equipment. Compute the depreciation using straight line method and depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life as provided under COA Circular 2017-004 dated December 13, 2017.
Below is the suggested pro forma adjusting entry:
Prior Year’s Adjustment xxx
Accumulated Depreciation-Asset Account xxx

We recommend to the Municipality to open a Bank Account for the Special Educational Fund (SEF), in compliance to the Section 4(b) One Fund Concept of the Manual of New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Unit (LGUs), Volume I and Sections 307, 308, 309 and 310 of the Republic Act (R.A) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

The Municipal accountant must set-up a receivable account for its expected revenue collection for a given year. The assessment on the real property taxes collections from the Office of the Municipal Assessor serves as basis of the receivable for a year. The budget officer should furnish a copy of the budget for the year to the municipal accountant for setting-up receivable, pursuant to Section 20 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
We recommended the management to enforce immediate settlement of the issued audit suspensions within the prescribed period as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

The State shall provide for a more responsive and accountable local government structure instituted through a system of decentralization whereby local government units shall be given more powers, authority, responsibilities, and resources. Toward this end, it is recommended the national government through the Department of Budget and Management should give a share in the Internal Revenue Allotment to the Municipality of Datu Hoffer Ampatuan to carry out its functions.
observations.managementAction: The DBM disapproved the LGU application for IRA allocations.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the
properties and equipment of the municipality. Also, require the General Services
and the Accounting Division to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true
and correct valuation of the said property, pursuant Section 156 of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 120 and
124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

We recommend the management to require the Inventory Committee to conduct
physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the existence of the balance
of the account Road Networks, pursuant to Sections V.1 and V.2 of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of
the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the
preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise,
require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road Map. A
Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at
the end of accounting period, in accordance with Sections V.3, V.8 and V.9 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

We recommend that Management instruct the Accountable Officers to strictly
comply with the preparation and submission of the monthly Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) as basis for monitoring the
movement of accountable forms at their possession to ensure reliability of the
monthly balances of inventory, pursuant Section 100 of the Presidential Decree
(P.D.) No. 1445 otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the period or date of acquisition or completion, as the case maybe, of the various assets and compute the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets. Prior Years’ depreciation should be taken up as Prior Years Depreciation Expenses Adjustment to be closed to Government Equity and current year’s depreciation should be recorded as expense.
The Municipal Accountant must submit a Report of Collections and Deposit for 2018 of Datu Hoffer Ampatuan and the corresponding amount DUE to OTHER LGUs i.e. province and the barangay concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted a Report of Collections and Deposit for 2018.

observations.recommendation: Settle the amount suspended in audit as soon as practicable otherwise it would mature into disallowance.

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to facilitate the tax mapping and updating of records so that a certified list of taxpayers can be prepared and be used as a basis in the recording of RPT and SET Receivables.

Recommendations
The State shall provide for a more responsive and accountable local government structure instituted through a system of decentralization whereby local government units shall be given more powers, authority, responsibilities, and RESOURCES. The process of decentralization shall proceed from the National Government to the local government units. Toward this end, it is highly recommended the national government through the Department of Budget and management should give a share in the Internal Revenue Allotment to the Municipality of Datu Hoffer Ampatuan to properly discharge its powers and carry out its full functions for its constituents and as effective partners in the attainment of national goals.

It is recommended the management should appraise the cost of the buildings to have reasonable value of the said assets and reflect the same to the corresponding records and submit immediately the Property Inventory Report for reconciliation with the records of the Treasurer as Property Custodian and that of the Accounting Records.
Settle the amount suspended in audit as soon as practicable otherwise it would mature into disallowance.

The State shall provide for a more responsive and accountable local government
structure instituted through a system of decentralization whereby local government units
shall be given more powers, authority, responsibilities, and RESOURCES. The process of
decentralization shall proceed from the National Government to the local government
units. Toward this end, it is recommended the national government through the
Department of Budget and management should give a share in. the Internal Revenue
Allotment to the Municipality of Datu Hoffer Ampatuan to carry out its functions.

It is recommended the management should appraise the cost of the buildings to
have reasonable value of the said assets and reflect the same to the corresponding records
and submit immediately the Property Inventory Report for reconciliation with the records
of the Treasurer as Property Custodian and that of the Accounting Records.

Settle the amount suspended in audit as soon as practicable otherwise it would
mature into disallowance.

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to facilitate the tax mapping and
updating of records so that a certified list of taxpayers can be prepared and be used as a
basis in the recording of RPT and SET Receivables.
Settle the amount suspended in audit as soon as practicable otherwise it would mature into disallowance.
